subject: Re: Fwd: Bullet Points
body:
PAY JOHN AND MACGUIRE

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> I met with John Amerling on Saturday before I left
> St. Thomas.  For  that 
> meeting, I asked John to prepare a one-page
> bullet-point list of issues he  needs 
> to have addressed.  I told him that, in the future,
> where he is  sending you 
> a multi-page narrative report in which there is
> contained a number  of 
> requests for you to respond to various issues, it
> would be much better to  include 
> such a one-page bullet-point summary of issues for
> you to address.   He told me 
> that he would do so for now on.  Regarding the
> bullet-point  summary of issues 
> that he delivered to me on Saturday, I told him the 
> following:
>  
> 1.    Regarding McComb's billing.  I told John  that
> John should be in the 
> loop for purposes of keeping track of cost of 
> McComb's services and evaluating 
> McComb's bills to make sure that they correctly 
> reflect the work McComb has 
> provided.  I told John that I would provide  John
> with a copy of our letter 
> agreement with McComb regarding his fees,  etc. 
> However, I told John that we 
> would make payment directly to McComb  and that, if
> John has issues with any of 
> McComb's invoices, John should not  handle those
> issues directly with McComb, 
> but let me know so that we can deal  with McComb
> directly regarding any such 
> issues.  OK?
>  
> 2.    Regarding John's role in connection with the 
> solicitation and m
> anagement of performance of vendors on the "Molyneux
> select  list."  I told John that 
> I would get your input, but that I would expect 
> that John should treat 
> vendors identified by Molyneux as recommendations
> which  need to be evaluated by 
> John along with other vendors so as to make sure 
> that the most appropriate 
> vendors are chosen for the work.  I told  John that
> Molyneux's recommendations 
> should be given consideration,  but they should not
> be the only vendors 
> considered.  I told him  that ideally we would like
> to have a competitive bidding 
> process utilizing bid  solicitations from at least 3
> sources, if available, and 
> including John's bidder  and bid analysis and John's
> work to refine each bid to 
> make sure that each  such bid is comprehensive and
> can be compared with the 
> other submitted  bids.  John's analysis should
> include John's recommendations of 
> what  John believes are the most appropriate
> bidders.  OK?
>  
> 3.    Regarding your working and billing
> relationship  with Molyneux.  I told 
> John that I would have to find out from you if you 
> want John to be more 
> involved in your relationship with Molyneux, such
> as, for  example, in the 
> tracking and evaluating Moyneux's invoices.  Please 
> let me know.
>  
> 4.    Regarding John's proposed approach for the 
> formulation of Utilities 
> and Services plans.  I told John that I would  let
> John know if you had any 
> objections to the three options he  proposed.
>  
> 5.    Regarding invoicing.  I told John to send a 
> copy of his invoices and 
> the invoices of those vendors under his supervision
> to  Rich and me at the same 
> time that he sends the invoices to LSJ  for payment
> (at FTC's address).
>  
> 6.    John will be submitting an invoice this  week.
>  He approximated that 
> the amount of the invoice for work of John's  firm
> through the end of April 
> would be $16,200.  He asked that if in  addition to
> getting that invoice paid, he 
> could receive a $15,000  retainer.  John's initial
> proposal never referenced a 
> retainer, but  John told me that his firm "will  be
> making some fairly 
> significant commitments of time and resources to
> this  project in pushing the permit 
> process", so he feels that a small retainer would 
> be appropriate.  Please 
> advise.
>  
>  
> Not included in the bullet-point  summary, but
> referred to in John's report, 
> was a discussion regarding  the accrued and expected
> fees and expenses of the 
> Maguire Group, the  engineering  firm used by John
> to document as-built 
> utilities and systems  conditions on the Island. 
> John wanted to make certain that 
> you were  comfortable with the money being spent on
> the Maguire Group.   
> According to John's report:
>  
> "The  value of the accumulated time for the Maguire
> [Group] is approximately 
> $4,000;  the expense of the [upcoming existing
> conditions] trip [to LSJ] will 
> be about  $3,500 and it may cost up to approximately
> $9,000 to then prepare 
> the base line  as built drawings and specification
> list."  
>  
> John Clarified for me at our meeting on  Saturday
> that the $3,500 is for 
> reimbursable expenses and excludes the Maguire 
> Group's fee for its trip to the 
> Island, but the $9,000 estimate of the cost to 
> prepare the base line as built 
> drawings and specifications list includes the 
> Maguire Group's fees for the 
> trip.
>  
> Based on John's report, the total  estimate of both
> accrued and future costs 
> and expenses of the Maguire Group to  prepare the
> base line as built drawings 
> and specifications list will be  approximately
> $16,500.
>  
> Darren
> > From: "John Amerling" <jamerling@viaccess.net>
> To: <DKIESQ@aol.com>
> Subject: Bullet Points
> Date: Sat, 6 May 2006 10:51:34 -0400
> 
> See enclosure. I will bring hard copy when we meet
> shortly. 
> 
> 


__________________________________________________

body_html:
PAY JOHN AND MACGUIRE

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

I met with John Amerling on Saturday before I left
St. Thomas. For that
meeting, I asked John to prepare a one-page
bullet-point list of issues he needs
to have addressed. I told him that, in the future,
where he is sending you
a multi-page narrative report in which there is
contained a number of
requests for you to respond to various issues, it
would be much better to include
such a one-page bullet-point summary of issues for
you to address. He told me
that he would do so for now on. Regarding the
bullet-point summary of issues
that he delivered to me on Saturday, I told him the
following:

  1. Regarding McComb's billing. I told John that

John should be in the
loop for purposes of keeping track of cost of
McComb's services and evaluating
McComb's bills to make sure that they correctly
reflect the work McComb has
provided. I told John that I would provide John
with a copy of our letter
agreement with McComb regarding his fees, etc.
However, I told John that we
would make payment directly to McComb and that, if
John has issues with any of
McComb's invoices, John should not handle those
issues directly with McComb,
but let me know so that we can deal with McComb
directly regarding any such
issues. OK?

  1. Regarding John's role in connection with the

solicitation and m
anagement of performance of vendors on the "Molyneux
select list." I told John that
I would get your input, but that I would expect
that John should treat
vendors identified by Molyneux as recommendations
which need to be evaluated by
John along with other vendors so as to make sure
that the most appropriate
vendors are chosen for the work. I told John that
Molyneux's recommendations
should be given consideration, but they should not
be the only vendors
considered. I told him that ideally we would like
to have a competitive bidding
process utilizing bid solicitations from at least 3
sources, if available, and
including John's bidder and bid analysis and John's
work to refine each bid to
make sure that each such bid is comprehensive and
can be compared with the
other submitted bids. John's analysis should
include John's recommendations of
what John believes are the most appropriate
bidders. OK?

  1. Regarding your working and billing

relationship with Molyneux. I told
John that I would have to find out from you if you
want John to be more
involved in your relationship with Molyneux, such
as, for example, in the
tracking and evaluating Moyneux's invoices. Please
let me know.

  1. Regarding John's proposed approach for the

formulation of Utilities
and Services plans. I told John that I would let
John know if you had any
objections to the three options he proposed.

  1. Regarding invoicing. I told John to send a

copy of his invoices and
the invoices of those vendors under his supervision
to Rich and me at the same
time that he sends the invoices to LSJ for payment
(at FTC's address).

  1. John will be submitting an invoice this week.

He approximated that
the amount of the invoice for work of John's firm
through the end of April
would be $16,200. He asked that if in addition to
getting that invoice paid, he
could receive a $15,000 retainer. John's initial
proposal never referenced a
retainer, but John told me that his firm "will be
making some fairly
significant commitments of time and resources to
this project in pushing the permit
process", so he feels that a small retainer would
be appropriate. Please
advise.

Not included in the bullet-point summary, but
referred to in John's report,
was a discussion regarding the accrued and expected
fees and expenses of the
Maguire Group, the engineering firm used by John
to document as-built
utilities and systems conditions on the Island.
John wanted to make certain that
you were comfortable with the money being spent on
the Maguire Group.
According to John's report:

"The value of the accumulated time for the Maguire
[Group] is approximately
$4,000; the expense of the [upcoming existing
conditions] trip [to LSJ] will
be about $3,500 and it may cost up to approximately
$9,000 to then prepare
the base line as built drawings and specification
list."

John Clarified for me at our meeting on Saturday
that the $3,500 is for
reimbursable expenses and excludes the Maguire
Group's fee for its trip to the
Island, but the $9,000 estimate of the cost to
prepare the base line as built
drawings and specifications list includes the
Maguire Group's fees for the
trip.

Based on John's report, the total estimate of both
accrued and future costs
and expenses of the Maguire Group to prepare the
base line as built drawings
and specifications list will be approximately
$16,500.

Darren

From: "John Amerling" jamerling@viaccess.net
To: DKIESQ@aol.com
Subject: Bullet Points
Date: Sat, 6 May 2006 10:51:34 -0400

See enclosure. I will bring hard copy when we meet
shortly.
